The '''Trest''' was a [[Casino/Legends|casino]] in the floating [[City/Legends|settlement]] of [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]], one of that city's three largest along with the [[Royal Casino/Legends|Royal Casino]] and the [[Pair O'Dice/Legends|Pair O'Dice]]. The [[Gambling/Legends|gambling]] tables attracted rich tourists, criminals, and even good citizens turned desperate by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="gg2" /><ref name="gg2-2nd" /> The Trest was frequented by various [[Species/Legends|species]], such as [[Anx/Legends|Anxs]], [[Bith/Legends|Biths]], [[Dug/Legends|Dugs]], [[Human/Legends|Humans]], [[Ithorian/Legends|Ithorians]], [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]], [[Rodian/Legends|Rodians]], [[Snivvian/Legends|Snivvians]], [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustans]], [[Toydarian/Legends|Toydarians]], [[Trandoshan/Legends|Trandoshans]], [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'leks]] and even [[Jawa/Legends|Jawas]]. The [[Gambling/Legends|gamblers]] could enjoy a variety of food and drinks, such as the [[Colt 45]] favored by the businessman [[Barpotomous Drebble/Legends|Barpotomous Drebble]].<ref name="ll">{{Tales|3|Lady Luck (comic story)}}</ref>

The Trest was itself an elaborate complex complete with restaurants and floor shows.<ref name="gg2" /><ref name="gg2-2nd" /> The main gambling chamber included many tables and a drink bar serving the patrons and the gamblers. A central pillar and the bar was decorated with boarded facing. The room had an overall [[Color/Legends|bluish]] hue and the two type of illuminants provided yellow light. One type of the illuminants were placed above every table, providing light for card-playing or eating. The other type was designed in a line-dot rhythm, and it ran along all the walls, above the bar and along the stair steps. Private chambers, like the [[Ecclessis Figg Room]] shared the same design except for the purple hue in the room. The corridors between the rooms were white and designed in the style<ref name="ll" /> of public corridors of Cloud City.<ref name="ESB">{{Film|V}}</ref>

[[1 ABY/Legends|A year after]] the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the professional gambler [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] visited the Trest and won a [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]] game against Drebble. Minutes later, Calrissian was invited to the casino's Ecclessis Figg Room to play against the [[Baron Administrator/Legends|Baron Administrator]] of the city, [[Dominic Raynor]]. In the first match, Raynor won his opponent's [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]], the ''[[Cobra]]'',<ref name="ll" /> but in the return match, Calrissian won the entirety of Cloud City.<ref name="ec">[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']]</ref><ref name="nec">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The Trest casino was first mentioned in the first edition of [[West End Games]]' roleplaying sourcebook, the [[1989]] [[Galaxy Guide 2: Yavin and Bespin]] written by [[Jonatha Ariadne Caspian]].<ref name="gg2" />
